intended_use: TruSPECT is intended for acceptance, transfer, display, storage, and processing of images for detection of radioisotope tracer uptakes in the patient's body. The device using various processing modes
intended_use_full: TruSPECT is intended for acceptance, transfer, display, storage, and processing of images for detection of radioisotope tracer uptakes in the patient's body. The device using various processing modes supported by the various clinical applications and various features designed to enhance image quality. The emission computerized tomography data can be coupled with registered and/or fused CT/MR scans and with physiological signals in order to depict, localize, and/or quantify the distribution of radionuclide tracers and anatomical structures in scanned body tissue for clinical diagnostic purposes.
target_condition: Low-count D-SPECT myocardial perfusion imaging (MPI) studies
ground_truth: Clinical routine high-count SPECT image (~1.0 MCounts) acquired under standard D-SPECT protocols; quantitative parameters derived using FDA-cleared Cedars-Sinai QPS/QGS software
other_metrics: LVEF correlation r=0.94; EDV correlation r=0.98; TPD correlation r=0.98; Bland-Altman mean bias for LVEF ±3%, EDV ±5 ml, Perfusion Volume ±5 ml, TPD ±3%; inter-observer percent agreement 97–100% on 5-point Likert scale (dichotomized ≥3 vs <3)
